title: RNA extraction of brain stem tissue for post-mortem diagnosis of rabies in animals by the LN34 real-time RT-PCR assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b0gm3","text":"Purpose ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c41q7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"basj","text":"To describe the procedure of RNA extraction on post mortem brain tissue specimen using the Direct-zol™ RNA MiniPrep kit (Zymo). warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9l27h","text":"Hazards and Safety Precautions ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"681nq","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a8cp3","text":"Wear appropriate personal protective equipment (gloves, safety glasses, and lab coat).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"769qs","text":"Follow procedures as demonstrated in the Biosafety in Microbiological and Biomedical Laboratories (BMBL), 5th Edition: (https://www.cdc.gov/biosafety/publications/bmbl5/bmbl.pdf)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"854f7","text":"Samples may contain infectious agent(s). You should be aware of the health hazard presented by such agents and should use, store, and dispose of such samples in accordance with the required safety regulations.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"80kan","text":"Pre-exposure rabies vaccination, regular serologic tests, and booster immunizations as necessary are required for all persons prior to working with lyssaviruses or with known or potentially infected specimen, or engaging in diagnostic, production, or research activities with these viruses (CDC, MMWR, 48: 1-22, 1999, WHO, OIE).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ikm7","text":"Some chemicals used with this assay may be hazardous or become hazardous; refer to the SDS as needed. Dispose of chemical waste as directed in the SDS and according to local regulations.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1k8iu","text":"TRIzol reagent is a hazardous chemical; contact with acids or bleach liberates toxic gases; ensure adequate ventilation; please refer to the safety data sheet for more information.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Frozen samples should be thawed just prior to testing. Clean the BSC work surface using RNase Away® and 70% ethanol prior to starting work. Layout plastic-lined absorbent work pad(s) in the hood to perform your work on. Place reagents, supplies, and sample in the BSC.

Thoroughly homogenize brain pieces using a bead beater, micro tissue grinder or sterile applicator stick. IMPORTANT: Ensure tissue is thoroughly homogenized. Incomplete homogenization will decrease RNA yield.
Let sit for at least 5 minutes at room temperature.
Centrifuge sample at 10,000–16,000 × g for 2 minutes

Transfer 300 μl of the supernatant into a new sterile microcentrifuge tube. Store the remaining homogenate in a screw-top tube at -20 o C. The supernatant should be clear, pink liquid similar to TRIzol reagent. Avoid collection of lipids and solid tissue. NOTE: For very little tissue (such as from a small bat), deteriorated tissue, or non-brain tissue, use 600 μl of the supernatant for extraction.
All centrifugation steps should be performed at 10,000 – 16,000 × g
Add equal volume ethanol (95–100%) to the homogenate supernatant (e.g. 300 μl ethanol to 300 μl TRIzol-supernatant) and mix thoroughly.

Transfer 600 µL of the ethanol-supernatant mixture to a Zymo-Spin™ IIC Column in a collection tube and centrifuge until the liquid has passed through the column (1 minute). Discard the flow-through. Repeat if there is more than 600 µL of ethanol-Trizol mixture for a sample.
Transfer column to a new collection tube.
Add 400 μl Direct-zol RNA PreWash to the column and centrifuge for 30 seconds.
Discard the flow-through, return the column to the collection tube, and repeat the 400 µL Direct-zol PreWash step.
Discard the flow-through and return the column to the collection tube.
Add 700 μl RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 2 minutes. Ensure that the wash buffer has been completely removed. Discard the flow-through and the collection tube.
Transfer the column carefully into an RNase-free tube.
To elute RNA, add 50 μl of DNase/RNase-Free water directly to the column matrix.
Incubate for 30 seconds at room temperature then centrifuge for 1 minute.

RNA is now ready for immediate use or storage. Keep extracted RNA on ice while in use. Carefully transfer RNA to new screw top flat bottom accession labeled microcentrifuge tube for long term storage at -70°C or colder.

Preferably, the RNA sample should be used directly after extraction. The sample may be placed in the refrigerator for short term storage. If a sample will not be used for a few days, it should be stored under long term storage conditions at -70°C or colder. Storage at warmer temperatures or repeated freeze-thaws can lead to RNA degradation and affect diagnostic results.
creator: Crystal Gigante
